    Accepted Papers

    RetroEval 2026
    Aberdeen, United Kingdom, 1-2 June, 2026

    Accepted Papers

    • Decomposition Does Not Help: Evidence from Semantic Clustering in LLM-based Causal Graph Discovery
      Nikolay Babakov and Alberto Bugarín-Diz

    • NLG Evaluation: Past, Present, Future
      Ehud Reiter

    • Evaluation and Assessment as Complementary Frameworks
      Elie Antoine

    • The Arabic Bible as an Evaluation Tool: The Case Study of the Khalīlī Arabic Dialect
      Jakub Zbrzeżny, Ehud Reiter, and Wei Zhao

    • RAG as a collapsed NLG pipeline
      Adarsa Sivaprasad, Barkavi Sundararajan and David M. Howcroft

    • A Comparative Evaluation of End-to-End and Pipeline Approaches for Summarisation
      Fahime Same, Saad Mahamood and Srinivas Ramesh Kamath

    • Solving the Task but Not the Problem: A Customer Support Case Study on Why Extrinsic Evaluation Matters
      Daniel Braun

    • Never Truly Out of Fashion: A Retrospective Look at Evaluation in NLG
      Patrícia Schmidtová, Saad Mahamood and Ondřej Dušek

    Accepted Extended Abstracts

    • Towards Grounded Evaluation of Multimodal Machine Translation Systems
      Sami Ul Haq and Sheila Castilho

    • The NL4XAI program: A retrospective
      Jose Maria Alonso-Moral

    • Ehud Reiter and the University of Santiago de Compostela: some notes and memories
      Alejandro Ramos Soto, Nikolay Babakov, Javier González Corbelle, Jose Maria Alonso-Moral, and Alberto Bugarín-Diz, Senén Barro

    • Checking for Implicit Assumptions in Data-to-text Generation
      Kristýna Onderková and Ondrej Dusek
